cobblemon.move.hellfireslash.desc: The user attacks 2 to 3 times with lethal slashes that can burn the target. Additionally, it drains 50% of the damage caused with each hit and greatly boosts both Attack and Special Attack.
cobblemon.move.electroears.desc: The user rubs its electric ears and launches a charged strike. Its power doubles if paralyzed, and its speed never decreases while attacking.
cobblemon.move.voltivore.desc: The user feeds on the kinetic energy of its opponent, recovering half the damage dealt to the target. If the target is paralyzed, the damage will be greater.
cobblemon.move.gmaxvolcaniceruption.desc: Causes a chain volcanic eruption that traps opponents between flames and rocks, setting Sunny weather.
cobblemon.move.gmaxwildforest.desc: Accelerates the growth of plants around itself, generating Grassy Terrain and Leech Seeds around the user, in addition to granting it Grass as an additional type.
cobblemon.move.abyssalrush.desc: The user charges wrapped in crushing abyssal waters, breaking all barriers and making the target flinch.
cobblemon.move.gmaxflood.desc: Creates a storm capable of flooding the battlefield, consecutively damaging the opponent while recurrently recovering its health thanks to its Aqua Ring.
cobblemon.move.berrytoss.desc: Throws its projectiles forcefully toward the opponent to strike with a powerful charge. The damage depends on the target's HP; the lower it is relative to its maximum HP, the greater the damage.
cobblemon.species.hichulla.name: Hichulla
cobblemon.species.hichulla.desc1: Hichulla loves to explore every corner of its surroundings, so it is common to find it in the wild.
cobblemon.species.hichulla.desc2: Hichulla has an innate talent for singing to such an extent that it is heard humming small melodies wherever it goes.
cobblemon.species.keiridol.name: Keiridol
cobblemon.species.keiridol.desc1: Keiridol's song is comparable to the voice of a goddess; the notes on its tail are synchronized with the melodies it sings.
cobblemon.species.keiridol.desc2: Keiridol's voice has the ability to hypnotize anyone who hears its song within a 50 km radius.
cobblemon.species.uynuk.name: Uynuk
cobblemon.species.uynuk.desc1: Born from the wishes of an artist to bring her works to life.
cobblemon.species.uynuk.desc2: Its edges are as fine as a blade of grass, so its cutting abilities are spectacular.
cobblemon.species.nucraft.name: Nucraft
cobblemon.species.nucraft.desc1: Nucraft has evolved into a three-dimensional body to share more artistic expressions with the world.
cobblemon.species.nucraft.desc2: Although its form suggests total mobility, it can actually only move its head, tail, and arms. To move around, it makes small jumps.
cobblemon.species.lilver.name: Lilver
cobblemon.species.lilver.desc1: Lilver loves to play with its trainer, although this can cause burns due to its poor control over the heat of its flames.
cobblemon.species.lilver.desc2: It is said that Lilver is the ideal Pokémon for detecting nearby fires, which has led many firefighters to use it as a guide in their missions.
cobblemon.species.goldy.name: Goldy
cobblemon.species.goldy.desc1: Goldy has better control over its flames, thus creating maneuvers to impress its trainer.
cobblemon.species.goldy.desc2: It is believed that Goldy's loyalty to its trainer is due to a spiritual bond that condenses in this Pokémon's flames.
cobblemon.species.lylaze.name: Lylaze
cobblemon.species.lylaze.desc1: It is known that the first known specimen of Lylaze gave its life to save its trainer from a tragic accident.
cobblemon.species.lylaze.desc2: They are peaceful and serene beings, so if found in the wild, there is nothing to fear.
cobblemon.species.ghissel.name: Ghissel
cobblemon.species.ghissel.desc1: Born in forgotten crypts, Ghissel wanders among tombs absorbing the torment of wandering souls, purifying them and storing the dark energy they release.
cobblemon.species.ghissel.desc2: Over time, Ghissel converts the impurities it absorbs into a spectral poison with which it seals the suffering of the souls it purifies.
cobblemon.species.schlanghoul.name: Schlanghoul
cobblemon.species.schlanghoul.desc1: Its head begins to show bony protuberances, mainly noticeable on its snout; this is due to the grieving souls it absorbs in an attempt to purify them.
cobblemon.species.schlanghoul.desc2: The crystal on its neck stores the souls it absorbs, which strengthen its body and make its poison much more lethal.
cobblemon.species.ghiftdorah.name: Ghiftdorah
cobblemon.species.ghiftdorah.desc1: The souls it has absorbed have completely mutated its body, making its soul sinister due to the torment it has accumulated all this time.
cobblemon.species.ghiftdorah.desc2: The erratic behavior of its side heads is due to the souls in its body fighting for control; however, when they coordinate, they are a lethal weapon.
cobblemon.species.hapu.name: Hapu
cobblemon.species.hapu.desc1: It often sneaks into power plants when it needs energy. It is a very active Pokémon, so it needs to recharge frequently.
cobblemon.species.hapu.desc2: Wild Hapu take advantage of electrical storms to recharge their energy using their tails as lightning rods.
cobblemon.species.strideer.name: Strideer
cobblemon.species.strideer.desc1: Strideer is capable of manipulating different types of energy through its antlers to brutally attack its opponents.
cobblemon.species.strideer.desc2: Sometimes it stores more energy than its body can handle, causing short circuits around it.
cobblemon.species.aaiper.name: Aaiper
cobblemon.species.aaiper.desc1: Aaiper's body became pure energy after an overload that caused ionization of its physical body.
cobblemon.species.aaiper.desc2: Being pure energy, Aaiper often sneaks into household appliances, causing them to short-circuit.
cobblemon.ability.divinevoice.desc: Its voice emits a frequency that boosts sound and Fairy-type attacks and protects it from powder moves.
cobblemon.battle.ability.divinevoice: The angelic voice of %1$s protects it from powders and boosts its song.
cobblemon.ability.corruptedsoul.desc: Converts its Normal- and Ghost-type moves into Dark-type, is immune to Intimidate and flinch, and boosts its Poison-type moves.
cobblemon.ability.precise.desc: Guarantees the maximum number of hits for multi-hit attacks, consequently generating more damage.
cobblemon.ability.illusiotype.desc: The Pokémon imitates both the form and type of a Pokémon on the team.
cobblemon.ability.illusoryember.desc: Adopts the appearance of the last Pokémon on the team upon entering battle. It can burn the target and recovers all its HP when the illusion is undone.
cobblemon.ability.sagacity.desc: Upon entering battle, the user's cunning intimidates the opponent so that its Special Attack decreases by one stage.
cobblemon.ability.chaosenergy.desc: The user's energy overflows to the point of increasing its Attack, Special Attack, and Speed upon entering battle and after each turn, in addition to increasing the damage of its attacks by 50%.
cobblemon.ability.grandencore.desc: At the audience's request, it has a 40% chance of hitting a second time.
cobblemon.ability.curiousinstinct.desc: Copies a random ability from all its opponents out of curiosity to know how it works.
cobblemon.ability.bloodsucker.desc: The user feeds on the blood of its opponent, recovering part of the damage dealt to the target. If the target faints, the user recovers all its health.
cobblemon.battle.heal.illusoryember: %1$s absorbed the embers of its illusion, recovering its HP.
cobblemon.species.kreti.name: Kreti
cobblemon.species.kreti.desc1: It is dedicated to playing pranks on both people and Pokémon, but don't take it the wrong way; it's its way of showing that it likes you.
cobblemon.species.kreti.desc2: Its flames are capable of creating illusions with such detail that they can confuse anyone who sees them.
cobblemon.species.kretsune.name: Kretsune
cobblemon.species.kretsune.desc1: Despite its mischievous nature, it is known as "The Prophet", as it is usually the voice of the divine beings with which it maintains a close relationship.
cobblemon.species.kretsune.desc2: It always protects the packs of Hichulla from any danger and stays close to Keiridol.
cobblemon.species.yurcat.name: Yurcat
cobblemon.species.yurcat.desc1: It is a species commonly associated with royalty and prestige, as it is the offspring of the proclaimed "King of the Depths".
cobblemon.species.yurcat.desc2: Even if you manage to catch it, Yurcat will not easily accept you as its trainer; you must earn its trust and approval.
cobblemon.species.marigueya.name: Marigueya
cobblemon.species.marigueya.desc1: It often climbs trees to camouflage itself from its predators, pretending to be local berries.
cobblemon.species.marigueya.desc2: It tends to cause problems both in nature and with its trainer. Don't be surprised to find your items scattered everywhere.
cobblemon.species.nancuache.name: Nancuache
cobblemon.species.nancuache.desc1: It always carries a Marigueya on its back and enjoys challenging Bug-type Pokémon despite its weakness to them.
cobblemon.species.nancuache.desc2: Its paternal instinct is the most admirable in the Pokémon world, only comparable to that of Kangaskhan and Dragapult.
cobblemon.species.arrkuro.name: Arrkuro
cobblemon.species.arrkuro.desc1: Due to its proud nature, it tends to disobey its trainer if it does not see them as someone respectable.
cobblemon.species.arrkuro.desc2: Its swimming ability and defense intensify, turning it into the second most skilled marine predator, only below its evolution.
cobblemon.species.zarinanjo.name: Zarinanjo
cobblemon.species.zarinanjo.desc1: It is capable of throwing small objects with such force that it can cause considerable blunt damage to whoever receives the impact.
cobblemon.species.zarinanjo.desc2: Together with two Marigueyas, it uses them as projectiles to confront its predators, rivaling them.
cobblemon.species.kurperor.name: Kurperor
cobblemon.species.kurperor.desc1: Kurperor is known as the "King of the Depths" for its imposing presence and longevity. Other Pokémon seek it out for its protection and wisdom in battle.
cobblemon.species.kurperor.desc2: Being a trainer of a Kurperor is a symbol of admiration and respect, as this species is only worthy of an admirable ruler.
cobblemon.species.aelubi.name: Aelubi
cobblemon.species.aelubi.desc1: Aelubi flies over the world on its mission to eradicate the evil that corrupts both humans and Pokémon.
cobblemon.species.aelubi.desc2: If you ever see it, show it gratitude for the protection it provides by offering something, preferably a sweet or dessert.
cobblemon.species.aelubi.desc3: It is said that Aelubi engages in intense battles against a legendary Pokémon of demonic appearance, in addition to trying to purify the minions of said Pokémon.
cobblemon.species.bellver.name: Bellver
cobblemon.species.bellver.desc1: Unlike its counterpart Lilver, Bellver is calmer and more relaxed.
cobblemon.species.bellver.desc2: Its aroma and sweet attitude transmit calm to both people and Pokémon.
cobblemon.species.belldy.name: Belldy
cobblemon.species.belldy.desc1: Its limbs become more robust, as does its bark, which significantly increases its resistance to attacks.
cobblemon.species.belldy.desc2: Despite its increased resistance, it is mostly a pacifist Pokémon.
cobblemon.species.bellaze.name: Bellaze
cobblemon.species.bellaze.desc1: Its tail grows until it becomes an apple tree bush, from which apples occasionally grow.
cobblemon.species.bellaze.desc2: The bush that grows on its tail is the perfect home for small Pokémon seeking its protection, as despite its calm nature, Bellaze does not hesitate to attack to help its friends.
cobblemon.species.byion.name: Byion
cobblemon.species.byion.desc1: Its spikes generate enough electricity to power an entire skyscraper.
cobblemon.species.byion.desc2: It usually lives in packs alongside Lilver; it is not known why, but their bond is believed to be theoretical.
cobblemon.species.byohm.name: Byohm
cobblemon.species.byohm.desc1: It can run at incalculable speeds and increase its speed and kinetic energy in a matter of seconds.
cobblemon.species.byohm.desc2: There have been reports of theft of kitchen utensils, mostly spoons; this species is believed to be involved.
cobblemon.species.mizuga.name: Mizuga
cobblemon.species.mizuga.desc1: Mizuga lives in warm oceans and coral reefs, where it spends the day playing among schools of fish.
cobblemon.species.mizuga.desc2: Divers say that its presence is a sign of a healthy and balanced reef.
cobblemon.species.mizuga.desc3: It is a peaceful and curious Pokémon that enjoys propelling itself with water to perform small charges without the intention of causing harm.
cobblemon.species.okamorph.name: Okamorph
cobblemon.species.okamorph.desc1: Okamorph's determination makes it evolve into this form that resembles a legendary Pokémon from a distant region.
cobblemon.species.okamorph.desc2: Okamorph's power becomes much more chaotic despite losing the ability to transform.
cobblemon.species.nefthlet.name: Nefthlet
cobblemon.species.nefthlet.desc1: Nefthlet usually flies over the entire region in search of ancient objects belonging to deceased people.
cobblemon.species.nefthlet.desc2: This species maintains constant disputes with Rookidee, as Nefthlet wait for a trainer to fall so they can steal their belongings, while Rookidee seek to protect them.
cobblemon.species.gotheneth.name: Gotheneth
cobblemon.species.gotheneth.desc1: When a Gotheneth perches next to a person, it is believed that their end is near, although it is only a superstition that few dare to ignore.
cobblemon.species.gotheneth.desc2: It serves as an emissary of its evolution, the Funerary Plume Pokémon. Avoid contact with it.
cobblemon.species.nenebris.name: Nenebris
cobblemon.species.nenebris.desc1: Nenebris wanders in search of fresh souls to sustain its existence for one more day.
cobblemon.species.nenebris.desc2: It is said that its appearance is an omen of imminent death, as its presence always coincides with the death of some human or Pokémon nearby.
cobblemon.species.buntari.name: Buntari
cobblemon.species.buntari.desc1: It can create perfect illusions that make it look like another Pokémon, even changing its type to that of the one it imitates.
cobblemon.species.buntari.desc2: When its illusion is broken, it envelops its allies with its huge fluffy tail and releases healing energy that fully restores their vitality.
cobblemon.species.tokomand.name: Tokomand
cobblemon.species.tokomand.desc1: We don't know how it stole part of Mimikyu's disguise without dying in the attempt; however, it wears a crown made of cardboard glued to the fabric with adhesive tape.
cobblemon.species.tokomand.desc2: Its attitude scares all the Pokémon around it. It is not recommended for beginner trainers to try to catch it.
cobblemon.species.tokarmy.name: Tokarmy
cobblemon.species.tokarmy.desc1: After countless events, Tokarmy now uses its old mask as a cape, a symbol that identifies it as a leader worthy of commanding all the Tokomand in the region.
cobblemon.species.tokarmy.desc2: It is rumored that Tokarmy has the ability to pilot a giant Tokomand formed by many Tokomand under its care.
cobblemon.species.nenevern.name: Nenevern
cobblemon.species.nenevern.desc1: Nenevern wraps itself in its large pink wings as if they were a cape and spends most of its time dozing, but if disturbed, it wakes up with an ultrasonic screech full of rage that confuses and disorients the rival.
cobblemon.species.nenevern.desc2: Its screech dizzies all Pokémon and humans around it; it is best not to disturb it during its lethargy.
cobblemon.species.nenevamp.name: Nenevamp
cobblemon.species.nenevamp.desc1: The humanoid form that Nenevamp adopts upon evolving is a reflection of how it looked before succumbing to the poison that turned it into a Pokémon.
cobblemon.species.nenevamp.desc2: Its vampiric nature gave way to a large number of immunities, and it now mainly feeds on the blood of its opponents.
cobblemon.move.comando.desc: On the first turn, it boosts the Attack and Special Attack of its allies, also increasing its own Evasion based on the number of allies on the field. In subsequent turns, it prevents the rival from using the move used in the previous turn. In single battles, Evasion increases based on the number of non-fainted Pokémon in the team.
